name: Cisco IOS XR Upgrade
description: Upgrade a Cisco IOS XR device using the install add/activate workflow. Optional SMU batches may be applied before and/or after the version upgrade. Restart type (reload vs process-restart) is auto-detected per SMU. Select target devices in the Job Template's Inventory or Limit field.

spec.question_description: Version string that must match EXACTLY what `show install active summary` prints on the Label: line after upgrade (e.g. 24.4.2 for a 3-octet release, 24.4.2.1 for a respin). Used in the post-check assertion.
TAR bundle or ISO filename already staged on the device (e.g. asr9k-x64-24.4.2.1.tar for a TAR bundle, asr9k-x64-24.4.2.1.iso for a plain ISO). Must exist at the chosen image path.
Filesystem where the image and SMUs are staged.

Optional. One SMU filename per line. Patches the CURRENT image, committed immediately so they survive a version rollback. Node is auto-isolated only if any SMU in the batch requires a reload. Leave empty to skip.

Optional. One SMU filename per line. Applied on top of the NEW version; committed atomically with the version upgrade. If any SMU in this batch requires reload, the node is re-isolated. Leave empty to skip.
32-char lowercase hex. Playbook runs `show md5 file` on the device and asserts it matches before `install add`. REQUIRED — generate with `md5sum {image_file}` on the file server.

YAML mapping of SMU filename → expected MD5. Example:
  asr9k-sysadmin-install-7.8.2.2-r782.CSCwi95358.x86_64.rpm: 6cb3873222ec7803ab7e2107551f3f5c
  asr9k-iosxr-install-1.0.0.2-r782.CSCwi95358.x86_64.rpm: 9fd66427f14de49eb7d651aca9abfc86
REQUIRED if 'SMUs BEFORE upgrade' is non-empty — one entry per filename, 32-char lowercase hex values. Playbook refuses to proceed if SMUs are listed without matching MD5s.

YAML mapping of SMU filename → expected MD5. Same format as the SMUs-BEFORE MD5 map. REQUIRED if 'SMUs AFTER upgrade' is non-empty — one entry per filename. Playbook refuses to proceed if SMUs are listed without matching MD5s.

spec.question_name: Run DISK-PREP phase (core file + inactive package cleanup)
spec.question_description: Cleans up core files under /misc/disk1 and removes inactive install packages on both XR and admin planes before the upgrade. Strongly recommended.
Disables `fpd auto-upgrade` and `fpd auto-reload` on both XR and sysadmin planes before the image install, so FPD changes are operator-controlled in the FPD phase.
After image install, run `upgrade hw-module location all fpd all` and optionally the sequenced per-card upgrades below.

spec.question_name: FPD admin reload required after upgrade
spec.question_description: Whether to run `admin hw-module location all reload` after FPD upgrades complete. Required for BIOS-class FPD changes.
Maximum wait for the device to return after a reload. Increase for large/multi-RP chassis.
Time between POST checks passing and `install commit`. Gives operators a window to observe and abort before the upgrade becomes permanent.
Where pre/post state snapshots are written on the AWX execution node. Use a persistent mount if you want them preserved across job runs.
